critical.mandate: User-initiated cross-sidecar reads are explicitly allowed per agent-specification-protocol
Never modify a sidecar file without explicit user confirmation for each entry
Preserve the sidecar file header and marker comment in all modifications
Process sidecars one at a time — release previous sidecar content before loading next (JIT)

step.title: Scan Sidecars
step.action: Read _kratos/_memory/config.yaml to get sidecar_root path
Enumerate all 9 *-sidecar/ directories under _kratos/_memory/
For each directory, read the .md file(s) inside
Determine if content exists beyond the template header — look for text after the <!-- Decisions will be appended below this line --> marker or similar comment markers
Build two lists: - Sidecars with content (candidates for hygiene review) - Sidecars without content (empty — will be skipped)
step.check: Report "All sidecars are empty — nothing to review" and complete the workflow
step.action: Report to user: "{N} sidecars with content found, {M} empty. Proceeding with review."

step.title: Load Reference Artifacts
step.action: Attempt to read each reference artifact (record which exist and which are missing): - {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — for architect, devops, security sidecar validation - {planning_artifacts}/prd.md — for pm sidecar validation - {planning_artifacts}/infrastructure-design.md — for devops sidecar validation - {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md — for test-architect sidecar validation - {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml — for sm sidecar validation
For each artifact that exists: retain its key sections and structure for comparison
step.check: Warn user: "No reference artifacts found. Hygiene will be limited to structural checks (orphaned references to nonexistent components). Create architecture.md or prd.md for full cross-referencing."

step.title: Cross-Reference Entries
step.action: Process each sidecar with content one at a time (JIT — release previous before loading next)

Identify individual entries within the sidecar by: - ### headings (ADR-style entries) - Date-prefixed list items (e.g., "- 2026-03-01: Decided to...") - If format is unstructured: treat entire post-header content as a single entry

For each sidecar, apply its specific validation rules: - architect-sidecar: Compare each entry against architecture.md ADR section. Check if the technology, component, or pattern referenced still exists in current architecture. - devops-sidecar: Compare against architecture.md deployment section and infrastructure-design.md. Check for changed hosting, container, or IaC choices. - security-sidecar: Compare against architecture.md security section. Check if referenced components still exist and threat mitigations match current security posture. - pm-sidecar: Compare against prd.md requirements and scope. Check if deprioritized or descoped features still have active product decisions. - sm-sidecar: Compare sprint references against sprint-status.yaml. Check for velocity data referencing completed or nonexistent sprints. - test-architect-sidecar: Compare against test-plan.md strategy and framework choices. - tech-writer-sidecar: Validate referenced conventions against project file patterns. - orchestrator-sidecar: SKIP — session log only, no decisions to validate. - storyteller-sidecar: SKIP — creative log, no decisions to validate.
If a sidecar's reference artifact is missing: flag all its entries as "UNVERIFIABLE — reference artifact not found" and note which artifact would be needed

step.title: Classify Entries
step.action: Assign each sidecar entry one of four statuses: - ACTIVE: Entry is consistent with current reference artifacts — no action needed - STALE: The referenced artifact has changed since this entry was recorded (e.g., architecture.md has a newer ADR on the same topic with a different decision) - CONTRADICTED: Current artifact explicitly states the opposite of what this entry records (e.g., sidecar says "Use PostgreSQL", architecture.md says "Use MongoDB" for the same component) - ORPHANED: The component, service, or feature referenced in this entry no longer exists in any current artifact
Record evidence for each classification: which artifact section confirms or contradicts, what changed, or what is missing

step.title: Present Findings
step.action:
Generate a findings report with: - Summary: total entries scanned, counts by status (ACTIVE, STALE, CONTRADICTED, ORPHANED) - Detailed table grouped by sidecar, sorted by severity (CONTRADICTED first, then STALE, then ORPHANED, then ACTIVE):
| Sidecar | Entry | Status | Evidence | Reference Artifact | |---------|-------|--------|----------|--------------------|
- Skipped sidecars listed separately (empty or session/creative logs)
step.template-output: Memory hygiene report with summary counts, detailed findings table, and skipped sidecars list.

step.title: User Action on Flagged Items
step.action: For each entry with status other than ACTIVE, ask the user to choose one action: - Keep: Add a [Reviewed: {date}] annotation to the entry — confirms user saw and accepted it - Archive: Move the entry to a ## Archived Decisions section at the bottom of the sidecar file, prefixed with [Archived: {date} — Reason: {status}]. Create the section if it does not exist. - Delete: Remove the entry from the sidecar file entirely

When modifying sidecar files: - ALWAYS preserve the file header (title, description, marker comment) - ALWAYS keep the <!-- Decisions will be appended below this line --> marker intact - Place the ## Archived Decisions section after all active entries but before end of file - Active entries remain between the marker comment and the ## Archived Decisions section
After all user actions are processed: report summary of changes made to each sidecar file

step.title: Optional: Prune Completed Checkpoints
step.ask: Would you like to prune old completed checkpoints? (yes / skip)
step.action: Read _kratos/_memory/checkpoints/completed/ and list all .yaml files with dates
step.ask: How many sprints of checkpoints to retain? (default: 2)
step.action: Delete checkpoint files older than the retention window
Report: "Pruned {N} completed checkpoint(s). Retained {M} within the {retention} sprint window."
